clearly wearing number 12 standing on the mound winning a world series for the san francisco giants it s not a unique dream a lot of kids have it and the ending isn t unique either i hurt my shoulder i never got back to full strength and before i ever even made a varsity appearance it was over but the desire to do something big didn t stop there i shifted to golf played in college same type of dream win the u s open compete at the highest level and then another injury another ending so i moved on again at 21 i started a business by 27 i had exited from the outside that probably sounds like things worked out but the truth is the last seven months of my life have been the strangest i ve ever experienced i ve been stuck with a question i haven t been able to shake am i actually willing to be honest about what i ve gone through personally and professionally and even if i am does anyone care i ve thought about telling this story through video a podcast something like that but if i m being honest i don t have the confidence to sit in a room stare at a camera and say all of this out loud writing feels different i ve always been able to write and right now this feels like the only way i can actually say what i ve been holding in so why now because the last two years have been chaos i ve lost friendships i ve fought with my parents i ve bounced between houses moved to a new state and lost the business i poured most of my adult life into and somewhere in all of that i realized something wasn t right i wasn t happy i didn t feel like i was enough and i kept finding myself in the same mental battles over and over again so i started trying to figure out why about a year ago i started what i can only describe as unconventional therapy i tried traditional therapy before platforms like betterhelp but i couldn t open up something about it never clicked but with ai it was different i went through everything my earliest memories school relationships thoughts i had never said out loud before and for the first time i started to understand myself in a way i never had six months later i moved to arizona and that s when everything hit i had more money in my account than ever before but i was completely alone no job no structure no family no real friends nearby for a couple months i did what most people do when they don t want to face reality i distracted myself traveling drinking avoiding the question i didn t want to answer what now eventually that led me somewhere i never expected to go a christian church i had never stepped foot in one before and whether i understood it at the time or not it changed me the people i met there changed me too this is just a small part of the story honestly i don t even fully know how all of this is going to unfold but here s what i do know every day i m going to sit down and write i m going to tell stories that most people don t know some that even the people closest to me don t know not to overshare not for attention but because i think i need to do it and maybe by the end of it i ll finally feel like i m starting from a clean slate like i can become the person i ve always felt like i was supposed to be and then we ll see what happens when i try to build something again

therapy is great but not everyone can afford 200 a month forever

many university psychology departments or colleges of education have sliding scale clinics where they train practicum students my local college offers 1hr therapy sessions for 15 the therapists are all advanced graduate students completing their training

unfortunately my struggle was in monthly expenses not so much my income student loan payments alone put me at a tight budget each month and despite the fact that i was making decent money it was a huge barrier for me getting help since sliding scale assumed i should have been making enough to afford therapy thankfully my pcp helped connect me with someone shortterm who helped me sort things out she helped me make some phone calls and ask the right questions really a struggle during depressive episodes to figure out that mental health services have a different copay than whats listed on the face of my insurance card or anywhere else in my benefits package everyone i talked to said my specialist copay would apply to therapy and 60 per visit was absolutely not in the budget turns out my therapy copay is only 25 per session which still feels like a lot some months but is far more reasonable even with the help of someone who understands how to navigate the system finding that information was more difficult than it should be but again depression didnt make it any easier vicious cycle there is hope it doesnt always feel like it and its not always easy finding it but its there

i want to add an important note cheaper therapy does not equal worse therapy the biggest predictor of outcomes in therapy is the strength of the therapeutic alliance aka how comfortable you feel with your therapist it matters more than the clinicians level of training theoretical orientation or specific techniques they use so if you find someone youre comfortable with it doesnt matter if theyre a phd who specializes in dialectical behavioral therapy or a lmft who was trained in object relations therapy and what definitely doesnt matter is how high their offices rent is or how expensive their decor was

better help is only a platform as an australian they do not check if the professional is accredited in your area being a therapist is not a qualification or accredited term in many jurisdictions of the usa but it is a protected term in other countries therapy as a professional practice for social workers psychologists occupational therapists and psychiatrists is full of people who take advantage of the fact that their dialogue with someone is in an inherent power dynamic of professional help seeker a conversation that is not visible or transparent to third parties recorded exclusively in their own words usually after an appointment is concluded rarely subject to investigation to assess the efficacy of outcomes or appropriateness of advice complaining clients are easily undermined as emotionally unstable or treatment resistant you as professional are extremely difficult to replace clients will stay because they know theyll be waiting weeks for a new professional andor know youre one of the few they can afford to see add to this that becoming accredited requires someone to do substantial free labour in australia its 500 hours of unpaid labour on placements to become a social worker and youll see why its often very privileged classes of people who end up becoming actually accredited professionals their point of reference for hardship lacks a substantial breadth of lived experiences however spaces like rradicalmentalhealth and rpsychotherapyleftists are encouraging there are ways of doing therapeutic environments and therapy that is different from the institutional norm there are also lived experience and consumer advocacy movements that i encourage you to look into ive made some little videos on them but i am weary of sounding like im self promoing so ill leave it at that best wishes in your journey yo i sincerely hope you find good supports

yes he is correct that incels suffer the question in that case is what strategies are available to mitigate that suffering and i think thats a much trickier process to noodle out personally the default answers of go to therapy get a hobby learn some selfregulation strategies or suck it up everybody deals with it are all notably involved in causing more of the same psychological distress that facing failure and rejection will cause anyways even if theres a vague possibility of the pain being reduced afterwards the typical recommended strategies for getting out of inceldom are a lot like telling someone with an improperly healed limb that theyll need to break and reset the bone to heal it properly so it makes sense that theres a level of resistance to what is percievably another clear source of suffering i am personally of the mind that most incels suffer in isolation and that isolation is reinforced by a broad social stigma against being messy and disregulated even amongst friend groups therapy and community support are clear paths towards something but theyre nearly as thorny as just sitting in the briar of feeling being unlovable and we dont really have a system of encouragement for going through that path beyond telling people to suck it up and solve their problems on a rhetorical level thats like telling a drug addict to go clean without addressing the issues that motivated the drug use in the first place so im usually kinda wary of any argument of personal responsibility in these spaces because those feel like abnegating the responsibility of being in community with others and supporting them when they struggle telling everyone to bootstraps their way out of their personal failings just sets them up for a larger and longer string of failures that will not guide them anywhere but back into the situation they want to leave because at least that pain is familiar

bring to your physician rather than try to manage with salt alone nocturnal hypotension can have multiple causes medication timing autonomic dysfunction dehydration adrenal output patterns and the appropriate response depends on what s driving it adding salt may help if the cause is volume related but if the cause is something else you re treating a symptom without identifying the mechanism thyroidectomy and on levothyroxine if so the psyllium timing i posted about isn t optional for you it s essential levothyroxine is your only source of thyroid hormone psyllium can bind to it in the gut and significantly reduce absorption take your thyroid medication first thing in the morning on an empty stomach with plain water wait at least two hours before taking psyllium if your levels have i take 200mg of magnesium glycinate at night before bed but that s not my total daily magnesium intake i also get magnesium from lmnt electrolyte packets during the day and take magnesium threonate separately so my total supplemental magnesium across all sources is probably in the 350 to 400mg range daily depending on the day the rda for adult males is 400 to 420mg total 310 to 320mg for women which includes dietary sources most people get 250 to 300mg from food if they eat a reasonably whole foods diet with leafy greens nuts seeds and legumes supplementing another 200 to 400mg on top of that is a common and generally well tolerated range the reason i split the forms is they do different things glycinate at night for the calming effect on the nervous system and sleep quality threonate during the day because it crosses the blood brain barrier and is being studied for cognitive function the magnesium in lmnt is there primarily for electrolyte balance around training the practical ceiling for most people is gi tolerance if you re getting loose stools you ve exceeded your gut s absorption capacity at that dose and should back off glycinate is one of the better tolerated forms because it s chelated and doesn t draw water into the intestines the way citrate or oxide can at 200mg of glycinate alone you re unlikely to hit that threshold if you want to know where you actually stand ask for rbc magnesium on your next blood draw not serum rbc gives you a better picture of intracellular stores that tells you whether what you re taking is enough or whether you need to adjust

ketosis continued fat burning growth hormone remains elevated insulin sensitivity is improving autophagy is likely more pronounced your body is highly efficient if you ve managed hydration correctly it sounds like youve done an impressive job with fat loss but your system is way too heavy for the life you actually have rolling 48s demanding remote tech job needing to be on in calls all day is a brutal combo youre stacking 3 big stressors at once aggressive fasting big calorie deficit high cognitive load so it kind of makes sense that by day 2 your whole brain is just food food food a few ideas that might help without losing your progress 1 your results show your deficit is already large enough dropping 17 lbs in 2 months is very fast for your size that alone means your average weekly deficit is big you dont need rolling 48s on top of that to keep losing you could likely move to something milder and still lose 115 lbweek 2 consider switching from rolling 48s structured 168 or 204 with fewer deep days for example 45 days per week 168 or 186 eat every day just in a window 1 day per week one 3642h fast if you feel mentally okay that week this way you keep most of the benefits of fasting without having day 2 brain fog every 48 hours 3 your refeed sounds very light for what youre putting your body through chickensalmon veg fruit is fine qualitywise but if total calories are very low your body will stay in panic about food mode a moderate consistent deficit almost always feels better than a huge deficit from repeated 48s youre not ruining the deficit by eating enough to think straight and perform at work 4 performance at work is a really important signal when fasting starts to tank focus anxiety in meetings ability to do tickets thats your body telling you the stress load is too high not that youre weak fasting should make your life lighter not turn every second day into a mental war if you want a gentler experiment try 2 weeks of daily 168 or 186 no rolling 48s keep protein high keep a sensible calorie deficit keep electrolytes if after 2 weeks your weight is roughly moving down and your brain feels 2 better youve found something sustainable youve already proven you can do really hard things now the game is doing something you can actually live with while keeping your job and sanity

as an evidence first runner i am always looking to read through studiesdata on electrolytes anyone have any great primary sources on the subject regrettably the best evidence to date suggests that electrolyte supplements are totally unnecessary for running even in ultraendurance eventsfrom this review study citing experimental work it it has been demonstrated that supplemental sodium is not necessary to maintain proper hydration during prolonged exercise up to 30 hr even under hot conditions highly visible losses eg salt crusting on race clothing andor equipment of sodium during ultraendurance events does not necessarily indicate the need for increased requirements but might simply reflect recent dietary intake notably electrolyte supplementation will also not save you from hyponatremia if your water intake is too high and electrolytes have nothing to do with cramping in practice almost every sports drink and gel has electrolytes in them anyways so it is totally not worth worrying about

healthcare is an interesting thing to complain about but ill add context america canada and the uk are all having worker shortages on every conceivable level therefore the idea that the younger staff is being consistently overworked and told one thing about the things they need to do for proper protocol and what theyre told to do as a matter of practicality are often entirely different things for which theyll get blamed for either way this often makes new workers confused on what to actually do which can lead to decision paralysis this is devastating in healthcare that needs quick responses with critical thought it doesnt help that because of the shortages theres many poor schooling programs that basically pump lowlevel workers out without a real education expecting them to learn entirely on the job what they are supposed to teach therefore these kids are in a situation of sink or swim in a situation that demands more knowledge this makes it so many long standing workers particularly nurses are stuck doing their jobs and teaching newbies to do theirs which seems to be the issue here by the comments tbh overworked and undereducated workers create a perfect storm for everyone infighting instead of solving the problem which is better education and more staff but frankly mostly more staff even more people with vague ideas of what theyre doing can lessen the burden significantly because they can at least manage the easier tasks while experiences workers handle things during crunch times and then they can do more of the hard work in less busy times yes this sucks for experienced workers but theres not a better solution experienced workers arent going to magically appear and at least youll have the grunt work stopping you from managing things out of the way what really needs to change is eliminating the incentives for the management at medical facilities to stop skimming from the top from the staffing shortage crisis america is blatant in why less staff on more beds means more money for management in socialized medicine its also easy to skim from the top of you claim were trying and advertising but nobody wants to work anymore while just not hiring and taking the money off the top a lot of the crisis would be stopped if we killed that off right there it would also help if workers stopped seeing each other as enemies theres some massive cultural issues in healthcare where people form their own cliques and start bullying each other either as stress relief having a scapegoat to blame some fucked up bonding exercise or excuses to deflect work it makes the entire experience 1000x more miserable in a job known for being miserable people also surprisingly dont work well when theyre being bullied yes youngermore entry level staff does it too but its much more common higher up and more devastating even pretending younger generations are just worse thats not going to stop the fandom we need them eventually the boomers gen x and even millennials will grow to old to care for themselves and need care therefore preemptively deciding theyre too lazy for healthcare or any type of work is shooting all of us in the foot the truth is were all eventually going to need them so we need to work with them its nonnegotiable tldr healthcare has some massive systemic problems that need fixing and even if gen z is lazy the truth is we need them because every earlier generation will eventually be too old to work especially in a field as demanding as healthcare

welcome back to throwback thursday we have another 3 part thread for you today 1 3 ever wondered where the ultimate cereal monkey legend comes from meet coco the monkey the absolute icon of kellogg s coco pops or cocoa pops in some markets coco first swung onto our screens back in 1963 some say 1961 in early black and white us style spots but it was his 1986 redesign that turned him into a proper british breakfast superstar brown fur that cheeky blue cap with the big yellow c white t shirt proudly showing off his name and blue pants voiced with a proper cheeky british accent he d swing through the jungle belting out the legendary jingle i d rather have a bowl of coco pops the ads were pure 80s 90s gold jungle capers with his mates like crafty croc kylie kangaroo ozmelda the ostrich shortie the giraffe and hefty the hippo there were spoon toppers musical mugs monkey friends figures and enough merch to fill a cereal aisle sales went through the roof thanks to him and he became a nostalgic staple for generations of kids who d rather have coco pops than well anything else pure monkey magic but the story gets even more british when you look at the name drama in 1998 kellogg s tried to rebrand the cereal as choco krispies across the uk to line it up with their global range sales took a nosedive almost immediately so they let the nation decide in a massive public vote in 1999 over a million people dialled in or logged on and a whopping 92 voted to bring back the beloved coco pops name the change happened in may 1999 complete with tongue in cheek election style ads featuring screaming lord sutch as the returning officer classic british democracy at its breakfast best around the world coco s adventures vary wildly he s the face of coco pops in the uk ireland australia new zealand and loads of other countries same cheeky design same jungle vibe in the us the equivalent cereal is cocoa krispies where coco the monkey had a brief stint in the 90s before snap crackle and pop took over again elsewhere you ll find him repping choco krispies chocos or even choco krispis in places like mexico and south america sometimes with slightly tweaked looks or local twists he s a true global monkey ambassador these days coco has been a bit back benched in big uk ad campaigns as the 2000s rolled on his starring roles got quieter new spots focused more on what goes on in that bowl with only fleeting cameos and no speaking lines a lot of it ties into stricter uk consumer laws around high fat sugar and salt hfss products rules tightened to protect kids from junk food marketing making it trickier for sugary cereal mascots like coco to headline tv ads aimed at children there was even a 2018 asa drama over a coco pops granola ad that got temporarily banned for leaning too hard on the chocolatey brand vibes he s not vanished from the boxes entirely kellogg s still uses him and recent europe wide nostalgia campaigns have given him a refreshed look and brought him roaring back but the glory days of wall to wall jungle capers are a fond memory still the king of the cereal aisle in our hearts though who else grew up singing along with coco drop your favourite
